nodes.label: Customer
nodes.detail: Customers have needs. The architecture captures them, routes them to the right place, and returns an outcome. This layer defines what customers arrive with and what they leave with.

nodes.label: Information Need
nodes.detail: Customer needs to know something — eligibility, status, process

nodes.label: Transactional Need
nodes.detail: Customer needs to do something — apply, pay, book, report

nodes.label: Support Need
nodes.detail: Customer needs intervention, assessment, or ongoing provision

nodes.label: Reporting or Notification Need
nodes.detail: Customer needs to tell the council something, or be told something

nodes.label: Referral Need
nodes.detail: Customer or third party initiates a referral into a service

nodes.label: Customer Outcomes
nodes.detail: What the customer leaves with

nodes.label: Resolved
nodes.detail: Need fully met — decision made, transaction complete, provision allocated

nodes.label: Partially Resolved
nodes.detail: Progress made but case loops back for further information or action

nodes.label: Referred
nodes.detail: Case passed to another service area, team, or external body

nodes.label: Informed
nodes.detail: Need met at information layer — no case raised

nodes.label: Appealed
nodes.detail: Customer contests the determination — internal review or tribunal route

nodes.label: Access
nodes.detail: The channels through which customers reach the council — in person, phone, online, email, letter, messaging, social media, or third-party referral — and the interfaces through which the council presents itself on each channel. Resolution outcomes and status notifications return to the customer through this same layer. AI operates across all layers, including here.

nodes.label: Channels
nodes.detail: How customers reach the council

nodes.label: Messaging
nodes.detail: WhatsApp, SMS

nodes.label: Third-Party Referral
nodes.detail: Inbound referral from another agency, GP, social care, voluntary sector, or partner organisation on behalf of a customer

nodes.label: Interfaces
nodes.detail: How the council presents itself on each channel

nodes.label: Human
nodes.detail: Officer, agent, or volunteer

nodes.label: APIs
nodes.detail: Machine-to-machine, partner access

nodes.label: Assisted Digital
nodes.detail: Officer completes digital process on behalf of customer

nodes.label: Information
nodes.detail: What the customer needs to know, and where things stand. This layer holds guidance content, a knowledge base, and case status. It covers eligibility and what to expect, policies and service rules, and updates on where a case is and what happens next. Some needs are fully resolved here — the customer gets the answer they came for without a case being raised.

nodes.label: Eligibility Criteria
nodes.detail: Who qualifies, conditions, thresholds

nodes.label: Process Guides
nodes.detail: Step-by-step what to expect

nodes.label: Plain Language Summaries
nodes.detail: Simplified versions of policy

nodes.label: Multilingual Content
nodes.detail: Translated and easy-read versions

nodes.label: Self-Resolution
nodes.detail: Need fully resolved at information layer — customer gets answer without a case being raised

nodes.label: Capabilities
nodes.detail: Where transactions happen in both directions. Customers submit forms and evidence. The council sends notifications. Payments and bookings are handled through shared components available across all service areas. Shared components are built once and used across all service areas — payments, bookings, and notifications are not rebuilt per service.

nodes.label: Digital Forms
nodes.detail: Structured data capture, conditional logic

nodes.label: Address Lookup
nodes.detail: PAF / LLPG integration

nodes.label: Orchestration
nodes.detail: Where the need is routed, tracked, and managed. This layer applies rules to assign cases to the right service area, holds a single view of the customer across all services, and manages service level agreements and contact history. The central customer record means every service area sees the same customer — not separate records held by separate teams.

nodes.label: Priority Scoring
nodes.detail: Vulnerability flags, urgency weighting

nodes.label: Performance Reporting
nodes.detail: Operational performance metrics against SLA commitments

nodes.label: Determination
nodes.detail: Where cases are assessed, decided, and resolved. Each service area has its own line of business solution — shared or dedicated. Officer decisions are made and recorded here: applications, approvals, refusals, allocations, assessments, and referrals. Outcomes return to the customer through the access layer. A determination does not always close a case — a partial resolution or a request for further information loops back through the architecture.

nodes.label: Partial Resolution
nodes.detail: Determination does not always close a case — partial outcome or further information request loops back through the architecture

nodes.label: Referral Out
nodes.detail: Case referred to another service area, team, or external body

nodes.label: Technology Foundations
nodes.detail: The infrastructure and standards that underpin all layers. Cloud hosting, security and identity, integration, data standards, observability, and verification — including identity, evidence, residency checks and fraud signals. These capabilities are not rebuilt per service; they gate and support the entire architecture.

nodes.label: Integration Layer
nodes.detail: API gateway, event bus, middleware

nodes.label: Verification
nodes.detail: Identity, evidence, residency, fraud signals — gates multiple layers

nodes.label: Verify Identity
nodes.detail: Document check, biometric, GOV.UK One Login

nodes.label: Accessibility
nodes.detail: Applies to channels, content, capabilities, and notifications equally

nodes.label: Document Management
nodes.detail: Shared capability for storing, retrieving and managing documents across services

nodes.label: Resource Management
nodes.detail: Inventory of bookable resources — staff, slots, physical assets — consumed by Appointment Booking

nodes.label: Find and Report
nodes.detail: User locates the relevant service or place, then submits a report. Common in environmental and highways services.

nodes.label: Prove and Apply
nodes.detail: User establishes their identity and eligibility before submitting a formal application.

nodes.label: Pay and Confirm
nodes.detail: User makes a payment and receives confirmation. Applies wherever a transaction closes a service interaction.

nodes.label: Apply, Check and Notify
nodes.detail: User applies, the Council checks eligibility, and the outcome is communicated back. Core pattern for entitlement services.

nodes.label: Book and Confirm
nodes.detail: User selects and reserves a resource, pays if required, and receives confirmation.

nodes.label: Track and Update
nodes.detail: User monitors case progress and can push new information back to the Council.

nodes.label: Order and Deliver
nodes.detail: User orders a physical item or document, pays if required, and the Council fulfils the order.

nodes.label: Report it
nodes.detail: A resident reports a problem — a pothole, a missed bin, fly-tipping — and expects it to be acted on.

nodes.label: Apply for something
nodes.detail: A resident applies for a permit, licence, exemption or entitlement from the Council.

nodes.label: Pay for something
nodes.detail: A resident pays money owed to the Council — a bill, penalty, or charge.

nodes.label: Book something
nodes.detail: A resident books a Council resource — a room, appointment, or collection.

nodes.label: Get support
nodes.detail: A resident establishes eligibility for and receives ongoing Council support.

nodes.label: Order something
nodes.detail: A resident orders a physical item from the Council — a bin, badge, or document.

nodes.label: Stay informed
nodes.detail: A resident finds information and tracks progress on something that matters to them.

nodes.label: Integration
nodes.detail: Cross-cutting integration layer mediating between Capabilities and Orchestration, and between Orchestration and Determination. Not a discrete tier — present at both seams.

nodes.label: API Gateway
nodes.detail: Unified entry point for service-to-service calls. Handles routing, auth, rate limiting, and versioning.

nodes.label: Event Bus
nodes.detail: Async message passing between capabilities, orchestration, and determination layers. Decouples producers from consumers.

nodes.label: Data Exchange
nodes.detail: Canonical data formats and transformation. Ensures consistent data contracts across layer boundaries.

nodes.label: Webhooks and Callbacks
nodes.detail: Outbound event notifications to third-party systems and back-office applications.

nodes.label: Idempotency and Retry
nodes.detail: Guarantees safe re-delivery of messages and prevents duplicate processing across seams.

edges.note: Human officer mediates digital process on customer's behalf
Customer gets answer and exits without a case being raised
Further information request re-enters at capabilities layer
Partial resolution may return additional status/guidance
Internal referral routes back through orchestration assignment
Structured referrals via partner portal or system-to-system API
Unstructured referrals via phone or email from a professional
Referral enters orchestration triage rather than self-service interaction path

changes_from_v1: Renamed layer 1 from Presentation to Access
Replaced Direct Digital / AI-Assisted / Person-Assisted components with Channels and Interfaces
Removed AI-Assisted as a structural component — AI retained as cross-cutting concern only
Renamed layer 3 from Interaction to Capabilities
Moved Verification from Capabilities layer into Technology Foundations
Added Accessibility as an explicit cross-cutting concern
changes_from_v2: Added Assisted Digital as Interface subnode (confirmed in PPTX, flagged from v1 diff)
Added Self-Resolution subnode on Information layer — need fully resolved without a case being raised (PPTX slide 3)
Added Performance Reporting as SLA subnode (PPTX slide 5 explicitly lists: service commitments, satisfaction, performance)
Added Partial Resolution subnode on Determination layer with loop-back edges to Capabilities and Information (PPTX slide 6: determination does not always close a case)
Added Referral Out subnode on Determination (PPTX slide 6: referrals are a distinct outcome alongside approvals/refusals/allocations)
Added shared_across edges from Shared Components to all three service areas (PPTX slide 4: built once, used across all service areas)
changes_from_v3: Added Third-Party Referral as a Channel subnode in the Access layer
Delivered via API (structured partner/portal referrals) or Human (phone/email from professionals)
Enters orchestration at triage rather than the customer self-service path
changes_from_v5: Replaced single resident_needs entry_point with a Customer Needs and Outcomes cluster
Customer Needs: Information, Transactional, Support, Reporting/Notification, Referral
Customer Outcomes: Resolved, Partially Resolved, Referred, Informed (no case), Appealed
Need types mapped to their primary serving layer
Outcome nodes wired to resolution feedback, determination subnodes, and self-resolution
changes_from_v6: Nodes reordered top-to-bottom matching SVG layout: Customer theme → Access → Information → Capabilities → Orchestration → Determination → Foundations → Cross-cutting
rank property added to every node (0=customer theme, 1=access, 2=information, 3=capabilities, 4=orchestration, 5=determination, 6=foundations, 7=cross-cutting)
changes_from_v7: Renamed customer_theme to layer_customer — Customer is now a proper layer (rank 0) alongside Access, Information, Capabilities, Orchestration, Determination
layer property set to 0 for all Customer layer nodes
changes_from_v8: Added detail descriptions to all layer nodes and foundations, drawn from PPTX slides and conversation context
changes_from_v9: Fixed technology_foundations node type from 'foundation' to 'layer' for consistency
